etymology.description: Variant of 雝 (harmonious) -> phonetic 邕 (sound) corrupted to 玄 (appearance similarity in ancient scripts) -> final form
etymology.interpretation: The character originally used 邕 as its phonetic component, and this contributed the sound. The corruption to 玄 over time was due to their visual similarity in ancient writing, influencing the final shape of the character.

examples.chinese: 家庭生活和谐(家庭生活和諧)，大家庭成员之间的关系很雍(大家庭成員之間的關係很雍)。
examples.pinyin: Jiātíng shēnghuó héxié, dàjiātíng chéngyuán zhījiān de guānxì hěn yōng.
examples.english: Family life is harmonious, and the relationships between extended family members are very harmonious.

examples.chinese: 这是一片雍容的田园(這是一片雍容的田園)，让人感到宁静和放松(讓人感到寧靜和放鬆)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè shì yī piàn yōngróng de tiányuán, ràng rén gǎndào níngjìng hé fàngsōng.
examples.english: This is a harmonious countryside that makes people feel peaceful and relaxed.
